items.title: Mo Salah announces he will leave Liverpool at end of EPL season
items.summary: Mo Salah announces he is leaving Liverpool after almost a decade of starring for the EPL giants.

items.title: Live: Australian share market rises ahead of CPI data release
items.summary: Australian shares have opened higher, after a volatile session on Wall Street where investors swayed between fears of rising oil prices and hopes for a resolution to the US-Israeli war on Iran. Follow the latest updates in our live blog.

items.title: Third man charged over the alleged abduction and murder of Chris Baghsarian
items.summary: Police allege the 23-year-old man was a "major player" in organising the abduction plot.

items.title: Football Australia vows high-quality Matildas opponents in lead-up to 2027 Women's World Cup
items.summary: After the highs of their run to the Asian Cup final, in just over two weeks the Matildas travel to Kenya to face teams ranked outside the top 100.

items.title: Three people killed, two injured in crash involving car and truck near Birdwood
items.summary: The sedan driver had life-threatening injuries and a fourth passenger was seriously injured.

items.title: Alleged northern NSW bank robber arrested after 30 years on the run

items.title: Labor says it is 'way too soon' to talk about fuel rationing — as it happened
items.summary: The government says it is "too soon" to be talking about fuel rationing as the war in the Middle East disrupts supply.

items.title: Russia fires nearly 400 drones in deadly attack on Ukraine, with UNESCO site targeted
items.summary: Three were killed and 30 wounded in a rare daytime attack, including on the city of Lviv, while four have been killed in an overnight bombardment.

items.title: Tropical Cyclone Narelle re-forms off WA, expected to reach category four
items.summary: Having cut a swathe across northern Australia over the past week, Tropical Cyclone Narelle is now threatening coastal communities in Western Australia.

items.title: Queensland wood mulch company accuses Ipswich council of 'political agenda'
items.summary: Queensland's waste industry has blasted a local council's "political agenda" over a decision requiring an odorous wood mulching site at Ipswich to be covered up quickly.

items.title: Wine growers fear smoke taint will spoil Victorian harvest as planned burns begin
items.summary: Just months after devastating bushfires, wine growers are concerned planned burns in regional Victoria pose a new risk to their grapes.

items.title: E-bike changes coming to NSW include reduced power and proposed age limit
items.summary: The NSW government has recently announced staged reforms to improve safety for e-bike riders and pedestrians. This is what it'll mean for the community.

items.title: What you need to know about Iran today, with Matthew Doran
items.summary: The focus of the Middle East war shifts to Lebanon as Israel plans to occupy southern parts of the country.

items.title: First Western Sydney Airport international flight tickets go on sale
items.summary: Singapore Airlines put their first international flights from WSI on sale this morning, confirming the airline will be among the first to run from Sydney's new airport.

items.title: The Malinauskas government's second term could be harder than its first
items.summary: Despite holding an overwhelming majority in parliament, the Malinauskas government faces no shortage of policy and political challenges to navigate in its second term.

items.title: Donald Trump says Iran sent US 'big present' as Pentagon readies thousands more troops for Middle East
items.summary: The US president suggests a mystery gift "worth a tremendous amount" is aiding diplomatic efforts to end the war, and comes as he reportedly prepares to send a major airborne force to the region.

items.title: Waste collectors warn bin services may stop if diesel not found urgently
items.summary: Waste collectors say they are struggling to acquire diesel and many are operating at "unsustainable" losses, putting the collection of household bins and commercial waste under threat.

items.title: Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke admits luck — not a plan — saved lives in Perth Australia Day failed bomb attack
items.summary: The home affairs minister says Australia was "so lucky" to avoid mass casualties during the attempted Australia Day bomb attack in Perth and admits the nation was slow to recognise the gravity of the alleged terrorist act.

items.title: NDIS participants work more, feel better after accessing scheme, report finds
items.summary: New analysis by the e61 Institute, which tracks NDIS participants over more than a decade, finds substantial improvements to their workforce participation and self-reported energy levels within four years of accessing the taxpayer-funded scheme.

items.title: Did AI really take their jobs?
items.summary: As big tech companies sack staff and say AI will pick up the slack, workers are left to grapple with what's really going on.

items.title: The Kates, Kate McLennan and Kate McCartney, take crime comedy Deadloch to Darwin

items.title: Travel quiz, March 25: Do you have the stamina to complete this trip?
items.summary: Which country has the dong as its currency? And where would you dine on tteokbokki?

items.title: Israel plans to occupy southern Lebanon from the Blue Line to the Litani River
items.summary: Israel's defence minister has announced plans for his country's military to take control of southern Lebanon, from the Israeli border — known as the Blue Line — to the Litani River.

items.title: Gold Coast Suns's Bailey Humphrey fails at AFL tribunal to get dangerous tackle ban overturned

items.summary: Brisbane defensive coach Ben Te'o, the mastermind behind the scrambling defence in the 2025 grand final win, hands in his resignation.

items.title: Labor caucus chooses new cabinet ministers as Liberals pledge election review

items.title: Brazilian man Marcelo Peres-Colhado, accused of being part of car theft syndicate, denied bail
items.summary: The prosecution argued the man was in Australia on an expired visa and could face deportation if granted bail.

items.title: Alex Robertson says Socceroos allegiance was never in doubt as he pushes for World Cup selection
items.summary: Alex Robertson says his allegiance to the Socceroos was never in doubt, as he pushes to earn selection in Tony Popovic's World Cup squad.

items.title: Australia's diesel standards lowered for six months as hundreds of petrol stations run out of certain fuels
items.summary: Australia is temporarily lowering standards for diesel to allow more supply into the domestic market as service stations across the country continue to experience localised shortages.

items.title: Oil price rises, Australian dollar falls as Strait of Hormuz remains effectively shut
items.summary: A key factor for shares, bonds, commodities and currencies remains consistently unchanged — the Strait of Hormuz is still effectively closed.

items.title: Organised criminals increasingly working with foreign 'despots and dictators', AFP boss warns
items.summary: The Australian Federal Police is warning that foreign intelligence agencies are increasingly working with locally-based organised criminals to carry out "serious crimes", including terrorism, within Australia.

items.title: EU leader urges democracies to band together in 'upside down' world
items.summary: The Europe Commission president declares that Europe and Australia must rearm and decarbonise as they try to break economic and energy dependencies on China and Russia.

items.title: Proposed Aboriginal land rights law change limits unused Crown land access, councils say
items.summary: Aboriginal councils who have fought for decades to secure ancestral land are concerned proposed law reform puts rights at risk.

items.title: How Bron Lewis went from dingy bars to hosting a MICF gala
items.summary: The crimson-haired comedian has gone from tiny open mics to the biggest stage in Melbourne in just a few short years.

items.title: Outback town of Marree receives anonymous donation of supplies after being cut off by floodwater
items.summary: An anonymous donor funded two planes full of supplies for the outback town of Marree, near Kati Thanda-Lake Eyre, after it was cut off by flooding.

items.title: Talia Gibson's dream US run ended by world number two Elena Rybakina in Miami Open
items.summary: Aussie Talia Gibson will leave the US full of pride and confidence despite falling to Elena Rybakina in the fourth round of the Miami Open.

items.title: Audiences consider The Diary of Anne Frank an 'uplifting' story arriving in Sydney at the right time
items.summary: While considering it a "big responsibility" bringing the theatre production to Sydney at this time, actress Chloe-Jean Vincent says Anne Frank's story makes theatregoers feel "inspired and so uplifted".

items.title: Longest-running abalone farm in the Southern Hemisphere to shut due to harmful algal bloom
items.summary: Fifteen permanent jobs will be lost on Kangaroo Island with the closure of Yumbah Aquaculture's abalone farm.

items.title: Pastoral company fined record $45,000 for Queensland biosecurity breaches
items.summary: A pastoral company has been hit with a record $45,000 fine after moving thousands of cattle across state lines without proper tracking. Authorities say the penalty sends a clear warning.

items.title: Georgia Voll hits Australia's third fastest T20 ton in victory over West Indies
items.summary: Georgia Voll puts her name alongside the likes of Alyssa Healy and Meg Lanning after smashing a 52-ball hundred in Australia's win over West Indies.

items.title: Beef and lamb exporters label EU free trade agreement 'simply appalling'
items.summary: The National Farmers' Federation says the deal will leave farmers without meaningful access to the EU market.

items.title: Iran shows Europe 'in reach' with longest-range ballistic missile launch

items.title: Influencer Stacey Warnecke's 'birthkeeper' may be compelled to give evidence at inquest, court hears
items.summary: A coroner investigating the death of nutrition influencer Stacey Warnecke after a freebirth may compel her support worker to appear at the inquest.

items.title: Japan urges public not to panic-buy toilet paper amid Iran war
items.summary: Japan's government releases a statement assuring people there is no issue with supply and no need to stock up, despite "reports and social media posts regarding the distribution of toilet paper".

items.title: Disability and chronic pain made motherhood isolating for Megan

items.title: Company that built highly criticised BOM website wins $16m contract for new site
items.summary: The company responsible for the highly criticised BOM website wins a $16 million tender to build a new climate data website for the government.

items.title: Donald Trump's backtrack illuminates Iran's leverage in an asymmetrical war
items.summary: In the wake of Donald Trump's backtrack, Iran realises more clearly than ever that it wields a potent new weapon on the global stage.

items.title: Donald Trump's Greenland threat likely to salvage Danish PM's political future
items.summary: When Donald Trump threatened to annex Greenland, Denmark leader Mette Frederiksen was flailing in the polls. Having stood up to the US president, she looks to have salvaged her political career.

items.title: Regional WA ambulance volunteers receive 'unfathomable' donation from patient
items.summary: A volunteer ambulance service in regional WA has received a donation from a thankful patient of more than $280,000, allowing it to purchase a new ambulance.

items.title: Hezbollah serves as 'pretext' for Israeli attacks, Lebanon's Justice Minister Adel Nassar says
items.summary: Lebanon's justice minister accuses the Iranian-backed militant group Hezbollah of effectively giving Israel an excuse to attack the country amid threats of an Israeli occupation of the south.

items.title: Eli Katoa's NRL future uncertain after head knocks, Melbourne Storm coach Craig Bellamy says
items.summary: Melbourne Storm coach Craig Bellamy says it is too early to know if superstar back rower Eli Katoa will get back on the field in 2027 or at all.

items.title: Diesel-reliant remote communities left vulnerable amid Iran conflict
items.summary: Remote communities' reliance on diesel fuel for power leaves them vulnerable in a global crisis.

items.title: Audio recording reveals moments after fatal LaGuardia airport crash
items.summary: Audio recordings from New York's LaGuardia airport reveal an air traffic controller appearing to admit he "messed up" just moments after an Air Canada Express regional jet crashed into a fire truck, killing the pilot and co-pilot.

items.title: England completes post-Ashes review as Brendon McCullum and Ben Stokes remain coach and captain
items.summary: The England and Wales Cricket Board has completed its review into a disastrous tour of Australia and New Zealand, with coach Brendon McCullum, captain Ben Stokes and men's team director Rob Key all keeping their jobs.

items.title: Australian Youth Orchestra shows the connection between maths and music at World Science Festival
items.summary: The Australian Youth Orchestra brings music, maths and science together at the World Science Festival, revealing how patterns and numbers are connected to the sounds we hear.

items.title: ASX recovers as miners, gold stocks rally, Australia Post jacks fuel surcharges — as it happened
items.summary: The economic shocks of the soaring fuel prices have continued, with Australia Post more than doubling fuel surcharges on deliveries for a wide range of customers.

items.title: Retirees grow native forest from scratch on Tasmanian conservation covenant
items.summary: Some become grey nomads, others learn a language or some other skill. After retirement, this couple took on creating a native forest. But, as John and Annette were to find out, there is "no textbook".

items.title: Women are less likely than men to receive CPR. This surf club is looking to change that
items.summary: NSW Ambulance data shows women are 10 per cent less likely than men to receive CPR from a bystander in public, and 50 per cent less likely to get defibrillation .

items.title: Trump looks increasingly trapped over Iran as markets gyrate and oil shortage hits heartland
items.summary: Donald Trump appears to be fast running out of exits on his Iran war folly.
